title: camissonia claviformis subsp. cruciformis
intro: Camissonia claviformis subsp. cruciformis, also known as the sand verbena, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Polemoniaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, and is often found growing in sandy or gravelly areas. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that have been used to treat various health conditions, such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which have been traditionally used to promote healing and well-being.
